summary: Iteration 3 full e2e test of Startup Master — Difficulty modal, Marketing panel, Stats tab (recharts), Fire staff, Prestige/IPO modal, Decision modal, save migration v1/v2→v3. Backend pytest 6/6 pass (unchanged). Frontend (390x844 mobile) entire flow GREEN with ZERO console errors. All 14 acceptance criteria from the review request were exercised and verified against persisted state in localStorage ('startup_master_save_v3').
critical_code_review_comments: useGameState.js has grown to 652 lines — close to the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into modules: state-initialization, tutorial, economy/tick, marketing, decisions, prestige. Not blocking but worth planning before iter 4.

chooseDifficulty preserves prestigeMult/ipoRounds via carryOver but the `tutorialDone` flag is only set when ipoRounds>0 — this means the first run always shows the tutorial even if the player picked a difficulty and immediately reset. Probably intentional (difficulty modal implies new run) but worth documenting.

fireStaff uses the role of the specific fired staff member (STAFF_ROLES.find by s.staff[idx].roleId) — correct, and severance=50% of role.cost is applied. Minor: if cash<severance the action silently fails via sfx.error() with no toast — user sees nothing. Consider adding a UI toast like 'Not enough cash for severance'.

Marketing action 'ads' effect only applies hype (+12) but has no revenueMult/durationMs → it never triggers the event overlay, whereas 'pr' and 'influencer' do. Consistent with spec; just note that the UI shows no confirmation for 'ads' besides the hype number changing. A tiny +€80 spent flash toast would improve UX.

DifficultyModal blocks the entire screen (zIndex 95) — no 'X' close button by design (player must pick). Good UX choice. Confirmed IPO reset also re-shows this modal via chooseDifficulty + setNeedsDifficulty(true) wiring in triggerIpo.
server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown') — carried over from iter 1 & 2. Low priority.
seed_data_creation: None persisted beyond the test run. LocalStorage cleared at start and between critical sub-tests. State injection used only for: (a) refilling cash=500 to exercise Marketing Ads, (b) setting pendingDecision='investor_offer' to render DecisionModal without waiting 60–150s, (c) seeding orphan v1/v2 keys to verify migration ignores them.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SAVE KEY: startup_master_save_v3. Clear ALL localStorage keys matching startup_master_save_* plus tut_done_shown before each fresh run. DifficultyModal blocks UI on first run — must click one of difficulty-{easy|normal|hard|hardcore}-btn. Selector CORRECTIONS from review brief: (a) Hello World launch button testid is 'launch-hello-btn' (NOT 'launch-hello_world-btn' — template id is 'hello'). (b) HUD language toggle testid is 'lang-toggle-btn' (NOT 'lang-toggle'). (c) HUD mute button testid is 'mute-btn' (NOT 'mute-toggle'). Verified values on Normal fresh start: cash=800, hype=35. On Easy: cash=1440, hype=55. On Hardcore: cash=400, hype=18. After tutorial, MarketingPanel appears and ads button spends €80 and grants +12 hype with 15s cooldown that correctly disables the button. Fire-staff registers page.on('dialog', accept) and removes 1 staff with ~100€ severance (50% of €200 junior cost). Stats tab renders 3 recharts SVGs inside stats-chart-panel plus 4 stat cards. Prestige ipo-confirm-btn is disabled when valuation<€10M; close works. Decision modal renders when state.pendingDecision is set; resolving it clears pendingDecision and applies option effect (investor_offer option-0 grants +€5000). Gem shop, lang toggle, mute all regression-green. 0 console errors throughout.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All iter 3 acceptance criteria met end-to-end.
